summary: Both integration suites are well-structured, cover all required test cases from the task spec, and follow the vitest API correctly. No critical or major issues. Three minor concerns are noted below.

issues.title: vault_not_deployed test: unused variable suppressed with `void`
issues.description: `developerBEoa` is extracted from `walletDeveloperB.account!.address` on line 753 and then immediately suppressed with `void developerBEoa` on line 771. The variable is never used in the test body. This is a lint-escape pattern that adds noise without value.
issues.recommendation: Remove the `developerBEoa` declaration entirely. The `walletDeveloperB` is pulled from `state` only to satisfy the destructuring pattern but its address is not needed — the middleware is already configured for developer B via `configB` built in `beforeAll`. Delete lines 753 and 771.

issues.title: Fastify happy path does not assert X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE shape
issues.description: The node-http happy path (lines 643-652) decodes and asserts the full `X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE` JSON shape (`success`, `transaction` regex, `network`, `payer`). The Fastify happy path only asserts the header is defined (line 703) but does not decode or assert any of its fields. This is an asymmetry: a bug where the Fastify adapter writes a malformed `X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE` body would pass this test.
issues.recommendation: Add the same decoded-shape assertions after line 703 as are present in the node-http test (lines 643-652): decode the header, assert `success === true`, `network === NETWORK_ID`, `transaction` matches the hash pattern, and `payer.toLowerCase() === signed.authorization.from.toLowerCase()`.

issues.title: Arc Testnet afterAll does not close the PublicClient transport
issues.description: The `afterAll` closes the HTTP server (line 224-226) but does not call `publicClient.transport.close()` or equivalent. viem's `http` transport does not keep persistent sockets by default, so this is unlikely to cause test hangs in practice, but it is worth noting for completeness. The analogous `forked-e2e` also does not close the viem transport (only the anvil child process and HTTP servers), but there the process death of anvil clears the connections anyway.
issues.recommendation: For the arc-testnet suite, consider calling `await (publicClient as any).transport?.close?.()` in afterAll after closing the server, or document the known non-issue with a comment. In the forked-e2e suite this is acceptable because the anvil child process is killed in afterAll and no persistent connections survive.

positives: Anvil spawn-and-kill lifecycle is correct: SIGTERM with 2 s escalation to SIGKILL in `killAnvil`, called unconditionally in afterAll. The stderr pipe and console.error on waitForPort failure gives good CI diagnostics.
TEST_PORT env var documented at top of file; default 8545; CI parallel-shard guidance present.

Cross-adapter NonceStore replay test correctly captures the raw header string from the first response and re-uses it verbatim to the second adapter, satisfying the spec's 'do not re-encode' requirement.
patchNetworksForAnvil correctly uses Object.defineProperty and stores the previous value for teardown, making the mutation reversible.

PaymentSplitterFactory deployed with the canonical 3-arg constructor (usdc, platformTreasury, initialFeeBps=50) per iter-3 addendum §1; vaultImpl read back from factory.vaultImpl() rather than being passed in.
Foundry artifacts consumed via JSON.parse + readFileSync with a clear 'run forge build first' error when files are missing.
Workspace package import `@universal-paywall/middleware` used throughout (not relative dist/ paths).
Arc Testnet suite uses `describe.skipIf(!SHOULD_RUN, ...)` (vitest canonical) with the `runCounter` structural proof that inner `it` bodies never execute when the flag is absent.
Canonical error strings match systemic-fix §4 exactly: `nonce_already_used`, `vault_not_deployed`, `paused`.
vitest.config.ts adds `testTimeout: 60_000` and `hookTimeout: 30_000` as required by the task spec, with a comment explaining the anvil and cache-TTL needs.
paused branch correctly calls factory.unpause() in a finally block to prevent state leaking into parallel reruns.
requireEnv helper in arc-testnet suite throws with a descriptive error identifying the missing var name and the required flag.
recommended_action: Fix T10-R1-M2 (missing Fastify response shape assertions) before merge; T10-R1-M1 and T10-R1-M3 are cosmetic/defensive and can be addressed opportunistically.
